title: Chief of Staff — personal operating system on Claude Code + Obsidian, framed as ADHD prosthetic
summary: A personal operating system on Claude Code + Obsidian, framed as an ADHD prosthetic. Five skills (/setup, /start, /sync, /wrap, /audit) drive a daily loop with persistent memory in .claude/memory/. Lived-in by the author across 100+ sessions before publication. Reference-grade documentation (~3,500+ lines of prose, ~200 lines of code). Adoption verdict: don't-port-from (different problem domain). Creator-lens classification: park-for-later — 13 knowledge candidates and 7 patterns/principles surfaced; 4 are port-now (rubber-stamp danger warning, search-vs-read tenet, tool-cost ranking + cheapest-tool-first, don't-batch-install discipline).
creator_view: Full creator view at .research/analysis/ai-chief-of-staff/creator-view.md. Six MUST-produce sections: What This Repo Understands (with blindspots), What's Relevant To Your Work, Where Your Approach Differs, The Challenge, Knowledge Candidates, What's Worth Avoiding. Note: an earlier draft skipped 4 artifacts under a 'JASON-OS doesn't use Obsidian' heuristic the skill text does not endorse; user reversed every skip and the rewritten view incorporates the full coverage. SKILL.md v1.1 hard-gates Coverage Audit to prevent the failure recurring.

candidates.name: Memory firings log + decay analysis with bootstrap gate
candidates.description: Append one line per memory-that-fired to a session-indexed log; decay analysis surfaces dormant memories; analysis gated on 10 distinct sessions to avoid false signal during bootstrap. Concrete cost: one append per turn. Applies when JASON-OS auto-memory exceeds ~30-40 entries OR stale-memory drift becomes visible. Source: docs/memory.md, .claude/skills/audit/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/wrap/SKILL.md.

candidates.name: Two-tier user-readable vs machinery file separation
candidates.description: User-facing daily-read files at top level (Command Center, To-Do); machinery (session counter, hot context, append-only logs) lives behind a single folder boundary (_system/). Applies when JASON-OS grows a user-facing layer beyond CLAUDE.md and SESSION_CONTEXT.md. Source: ARCHITECTURE.md §Technical, docs/protocols.md.

candidates.name: /audit skill as scheduled aggregate cross-system check
candidates.description: Read-mostly periodic check (every 7 sessions). Six dimensions: redundant memory, stale memory, MEMORY.md index drift, broken wikilinks, skill-vs-system drift, gated decay analysis. Surfaces findings; user picks fixes. Applies when JASON-OS skill or rule count grows past ~20 and aggregate drift becomes invisible. Source: .claude/skills/audit/SKILL.md.

candidates.name: Setup skill as runnable first-time interview
candidates.description: Skippable conversational interview that ships a working system in 10 minutes; minimum 4 questions then explicit ship-it-or-keep-going gate. Edge cases for input variations handled inline. Applies if JASON-OS ships as a clone-and-run template (not a current goal). Source: .claude/skills/setup/SKILL.md.

candidates.name: Use-both / bridge-via-Claude-MCP architecture pattern
candidates.description: Personal layer in tool A + collaborative layer in tool B + Claude reading both via MCP. Maps onto SoNash + JASON-OS + cross-repo-movement-reframe split as a shape reference for how to think about the architecture. Source: docs/notion-vs-obsidian.md, docs/notion-vs-obsidian-deep.md.

candidates.name: ADAM coherence_monitor.py reading session JSONL for compaction events
candidates.description: Read Claude Code JSONL session files; detect compaction events; write alerts. JASON-OS has compact-restore.js as part of session-start hook layer. Pattern is to MONITOR session JSONL for state changes. Worth a future /repo-analysis on the ADAM repo itself. Source: CREDITS.md (script not shipped in this repo).

candidates.name: Dual-mode docs (human + LLM coach)
candidates.description: README written so a human reads it as install steps OR pastes it into Claude and Claude walks them through. obsidian-setup.md has 'For the human reading this' section followed by 'Below the line is Claude's operating manual.' Applies if/when JASON-OS produces public-facing documentation. Source: README.md, docs/obsidian-setup.md.

candidates.name: Rubber-stamp danger for AI-drafted feedback memories
candidates.description: When the model drafts a feedback memory after a correction, the same model that just made the mistake is now writing the rule meant to prevent it. Review like an intern wrote it; reject anything that sounds self-exculpating; tighten rules that try to cover more ground than the moment justified. Port-now: add one sentence to JASON-OS auto-memory writing instructions in CLAUDE.md (or a tenet memory). Source: docs/feedback-loop.md.

candidates.name: Search-vs-read structural design tenet
candidates.description: AI working through search-API-mediated workspace degrades on cross-source synthesis; AI with direct file-system access handles it. Articulates the access-pattern angle of JASON-OS's existing filesystem-verification posture. Port-now: pair with tenet_filesystem_verification.md or extend that tenet. Source: docs/notion-vs-obsidian.md, docs/notion-vs-obsidian-deep.md.

candidates.name: Tool-cost ranking + cheapest-tool-first rule
candidates.description: Ranking: raw file read < CLI shell exec < eval-via-CLI < MCP server. MCP servers cost tokens before any call (schemas load up-front). Rule: 'raw reads for known paths, CLI for search and listings, semantic only for cross-domain, MCP only when you need rendered view.' JASON-OS has ToolSearch for deferred MCP tools but no stated principle. Port-now: tenet candidate. Source: docs/obsidian-setup.md Layer 5.

candidates.name: Format-longevity / plain-text-outlives-the-tool
candidates.description: Plain-text markdown is readable in any text editor in 30 years; proprietary databases aren't. Asymmetric migration pain. JASON-OS lives this implicitly; doesn't articulate it. Port-when-needed: tenet candidate when a substrate decision is up for debate. Source: docs/notion-vs-obsidian-deep.md.

candidates.name: Compact four-laws crystallization as CLAUDE.md frame
candidates.description: Four operating laws (Operating Constraint, Verification Protocol, Scope Discipline, Execute-Don't-Meta-Talk) with rationale and copy-pasteable instruction blocks. Compactness contrasts with JASON-OS CLAUDE.md §4's 16 behavioral guardrails — comparison data for a future trim pass. Source: docs/laws.md.

candidates.name: Don't-batch-install operational discipline
candidates.description: 'Add these one at a time. Verify each one works before adding the next. Never batch-install MCP servers and hope they all come up.' Generalizes to skill-porting, hook-adding, dependency-introduction. Port-now: add as cross-cutting rule in JASON-OS conventions or CLAUDE.md §4. Source: docs/obsidian-setup.md Layer 4.

candidates.name: CREDITS.md as a maintained attribution protocol
candidates.description: Written rules for when attribution gets added; per-file docstring requirements for code-adapted vs concept-adapted; full MIT copyright + URL + path on code-adapted. Port-when-needed: when JASON-OS ↔ SoNash cross-pollination gets dense enough that informal attribution stops working. Source: CREDITS.md.

candidates.name: Markdown-as-substrate scaling pattern (plugin-ecosystem inspiration)
candidates.description: Obsidian plugin ecosystem (Dataview, Smart Connections, Templater, Tasks) demonstrates that typed-markdown corpus with frontmatter has more depth than 'a folder of notes': declarative queries, local-embedding semantic layer, logic-in-templates. Existence proof of where markdown-substrate systems can grow. Inspiration only; no port. Source: docs/obsidian-setup.md Layer 2.

candidates.name: Editor-as-CLI tool-tier insight
candidates.description: Author's hard-won lesson: 'I spent weeks doing MCP gymnastics that the CLI would have replaced in a single command.' Validates the cheapest-tool-first principle from a different angle. Source: docs/obsidian-setup.md Layer 3.

candidates.name: Memory has decay; needs scheduled review with bootstrap gate
candidates.description: Memory rules go stale. Two mechanisms: ambient (when a memory fires and the user disagrees, the rule gets revisited) + scheduled (every N sessions, surface dormant memories). Bootstrap gate prevents false-positive dormant flags during initial accumulation. Pairs conceptually with the firings-log pattern above. Source: docs/memory.md, docs/feedback-loop.md.

candidates.name: Adjacent-implementations listing as research-honesty discipline
candidates.description: Naming peer projects with rationale per project so 'the field is named rather than strawmanned.' Inspirational shape for any future research-output framing. Source: CREDITS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md.
